name: St. Patrick's Day
description: St. Patrick, Ireland's patron saint, and the traditions of St. Patrick's Day

questions.q: On what date is St. Patrick's Day celebrated?

source.notes: Marks the traditional death date of St. Patrick in 461 AD.

questions.q: What three-leafed plant is St. Patrick said to have used to teach the Holy Trinity?

source.notes: Three leaves representing Father, Son, and Holy Spirit.

questions.q: What color is most strongly associated with St. Patrick's Day?

source.notes: Historically 'St. Patrick's blue' was the color, before green overtook it.

questions.q: What small mythical Irish creature is famously linked to St. Patrick's Day?

source.notes: A mischievous fairy shoemaker in Irish folklore.

questions.q: What is said to be at the end of every rainbow in Irish folklore?

source.notes: Hidden by a leprechaun.

questions.q: Which U.S. city has dyed its river green for St. Patrick's Day since 1962?

source.notes: Tradition started by Stephen Bailey and the plumbers' union.

questions.q: What Irish-American stew is traditionally eaten on St. Patrick's Day in the United States?

source.notes: An Irish-American creation, not a traditional Irish dish.

questions.q: What was St. Patrick's birth name, before he took the name Patrick?

source.notes: Took the Latin name Patricius after becoming a priest.

questions.q: In which country was St. Patrick actually born?

source.notes: In Roman Britain in the late 4th century AD.

questions.q: What does the Irish phrase 'Erin go Bragh' mean?

source.notes: Anglicized form of 'Éirinn go Brách.'

questions.q: In what year was St. Patrick's Day declared an official public holiday in Ireland?

source.notes: Enacted by the Bank Holiday (Ireland) Act 1903.

questions.q: Which U.S. city held the first recorded St. Patrick's Day parade, in 1737?

source.notes: Organized by the Charitable Irish Society.

questions.q: How old was St. Patrick, according to his Confessio, when Irish raiders kidnapped him?

source.notes: He was enslaved for six years before escaping.

questions.q: What is the traditional Irish word for whiskey, literally meaning 'water of life'?

source.notes: Anglicized over time into 'whiskey.'

questions.q: What traditional Irish frame drum is often heard in Irish folk music?

source.notes: Played with a two-headed stick called a tipper or cipín.

questions.q: What is the name of the famous stone at Blarney Castle, said to grant the gift of eloquence when kissed?

source.notes: Set into the battlements of Blarney Castle in County Cork.

questions.q: Which famous Irish illuminated manuscript is displayed at Trinity College Dublin?

source.notes: Dates from around 800 AD.

questions.q: What is the capital of Ireland?

source.notes: Hosts Ireland's largest official St. Patrick's Day Festival.

questions.q: Which Irish city claims to host the oldest St. Patrick's Day parade in Ireland itself?

source.notes: First held in 1903.

questions.q: In which year was the first St. Patrick's Day parade held in New York City?

source.notes: Organized by Irish soldiers serving in the British Army.

questions.q: Which ancient Gaelic field sport played with a hurley stick is considered one of the world's oldest team sports?

source.notes: Inscribed on UNESCO's list of Intangible Cultural Heritage in 2018.

questions.q: What is Ireland's highest mountain, located in County Kerry?

source.notes: 1,038 meters tall; part of the Macgillycuddy's Reeks range.
